subject: Mathematics
title: The Banach-Tarski Paradox
description: A quick win: understand The Banach-Tarski Paradox.

hook.text: Take a solid ball. Cut it into five pieces. Reassemble them into two balls, each the same size.

buildup.text: This isn't a magic trick—it's a proven theorem in set theory using the Axiom of Choice.

discovery.text: The pieces aren't ordinary shapes. They're infinitely complex, non-measurable sets.

twist.text: It only works in theory. The pieces can't be physically constructed—they exist only in abstract math.

climax.text: The paradox shows that the Axiom of Choice allows mathematically valid but physically absurd results.

punchline.text: Math says you can double a ball. Physics says good luck trying.

quiz.question: What mathematical axiom makes the Banach-Tarski Paradox possible?
